811.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'Whoever recites the last two ayahs of Surah al-Baqarah at night, they will suffice him.'
811.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa yang membaca dua ayat terakhir Surah al-Baqarah di malam hari, keduanya akan mencukupinya.'

812.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'Allah has not given permission for anything as He has given permission for a prophet to recite the Quran melodiously in a raised voice.'
812.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Allah tidak mengizinkan sesuatu sebagaimana Dia mengizinkan seorang nabi untuk membaca Al-Quran dengan merdu dan suara yang diangkat.'

813.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Prophet ﷺ heard Abu Musa reciting and said: 'This man has been given one of the flutes of the family of David.'
813.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Nabi ﷺ mendengar Abu Musa membaca dan bersabda: 'Orang ini telah diberi salah satu seruling dari keluarga Dawud.'

814.iwh_en: It was narrated from Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'He is not one of us who does not recite the Quran melodiously.'
814.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Dia bukan termasuk golongan kami yang tidak membaca Al-Quran dengan merdu.'

815.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'Allah has not given permission for anything as He has given permission for a prophet to recite the Quran melodiously.'
815.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Allah tidak mengizinkan sesuatu sebagaimana Dia mengizinkan seorang nabi untuk membaca Al-Quran dengan merdu.'

816.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ recited the verse: 'Respond to Allah and to the Messenger when he calls you' (al-Anfal: 24), then said: 'Shall I not teach you the greatest surah of the Quran before I leave the mosque?' When he intended to leave, he said: 'Al-hamdu lillahi rabbil-alamin (Surah al-Fatihah) — it is the seven oft-repeated verses and the Magnificent Quran that I was given.'
816.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ membaca ayat: 'Penuhilah seruan Allah dan Rasul apabila dia menyeru kalian' (al-Anfal: 24), kemudian bersabda: 'Tidakkah aku ajarkan kepadamu surah terbesar Al-Quran sebelum aku meninggalkan masjid?' Ketika beliau hendak pergi, beliau bersabda: 'Al-hamdu lillahi rabbil-alamin (Surah al-Fatihah) — itulah tujuh ayat yang sering diulang dan Al-Quran yang agung yang diberikan kepadaku.'

817.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Amr (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'He who recites the Quran in less than three days does not properly understand it.'
817.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Amr (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Orang yang membaca Al-Quran dalam kurang dari tiga hari tidak benar-benar memahaminya.'

818.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'When the adhan is called, the devil flees with winds breaking until he cannot hear the adhan. When the adhan is over, he returns. When the iqamah is called, he flees again. When it is over, he returns to distract the man from his prayer.'
818.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Ketika adzan dikumandangkan, setan lari sambil mengeluarkan angin hingga tidak dapat mendengar adzan. Ketika adzan selesai, ia kembali. Ketika iqamah dikumandangkan, ia lari lagi. Ketika selesai, ia kembali untuk mengganggu seseorang dari shalatnya.'

819.iwh_en: It was narrated that if a man prayed four rak'ahs and forgot whether he had prayed four or five, he should stand and pray one more rak'ah, then after that prostrate twice. If he had prayed five, they would make his prayer even; and if he had prayed four, they would be a humiliation for the devil.
819.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan bahwa jika seseorang shalat empat rakaat dan lupa apakah ia telah shalat empat atau lima, hendaklah ia berdiri dan shalat satu rakaat lagi, kemudian setelah itu bersujud dua kali. Jika ia telah shalat lima rakaat, keduanya akan menjadikan shalatnya genap; dan jika ia telah shalat empat rakaat, keduanya akan menjadi kehinaan bagi setan.

820.iwh_en: It was narrated that the Prophet ﷺ prayed with them and then stood, when they said: 'The prayer has been shortened, the prayer has been shortened.' Among the people were Abu Bakr and Umar, but they did not speak. Among the people was a man with long arms called Dhu al-Yadayn, who said: 'O Messenger of Allah, did you forget the prayer or was it shortened?'
820.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ shalat bersama mereka kemudian berdiri, ketika mereka berkata: 'Shalat telah dipersingkat, shalat telah dipersingkat.' Di antara mereka ada Abu Bakr dan Umar, tetapi mereka tidak berbicara. Di antara mereka ada seorang laki-laki bertangan panjang bernama Dhu al-Yadayn, yang berkata: 'Wahai Rasulullah, apakah engkau lupa shalat ataukah dipersingkat?'

821.iwh_en: It was narrated that Dhu al-Shimalayn, ally of Banu Zuhrah, asked: 'Has it been shortened or have you forgotten, O Messenger of Allah?' The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'I have not forgotten and it has not been shortened.' Dhu al-Shimalayn said: 'But some of that happened.' So the Prophet confirmed with Abu Bakr and Umar, then returned and completed the prayer, then prostrated twice for the lapse.
821.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan bahwa Dhu al-Shimalayn, sekutu Banu Zuhrah, bertanya: 'Apakah dipersingkat atau engkau lupa, wahai Rasulullah?' Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Aku tidak lupa dan tidak dipersingkat.' Dhu al-Shimalayn berkata: 'Tetapi sebagian dari itu terjadi.' Maka Nabi memastikan dengan Abu Bakr dan Umar, kemudian kembali dan menyelesaikan shalat, kemudian bersujud dua kali karena kelalaian.

822.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ prayed Dhuhr five rak'ahs. He was asked: 'Was the prayer increased?' He said: 'Why do you ask?' They said: 'You prayed five.' So he turned and prostrated twice.
822.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ shalat Dzhuhur lima rakaat. Beliau ditanya: 'Apakah shalat ditambah?' Beliau berkata: 'Mengapa kalian bertanya?' Mereka berkata: 'Engkau shalat lima rakaat.' Maka beliau berpaling dan bersujud dua kali.

823.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Buhaynah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ led us in two rak'ahs then stood without sitting, and the people stood (with him). When he finished the prayer and we waited for the salam, he said the takbir while still seated and prostrated twice before the salam, then gave the salam.'
823.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Buhaynah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ mengimami kami dua rakaat kemudian berdiri tanpa duduk, dan orang-orang pun berdiri (bersamanya). Ketika ia menyelesaikan shalat dan kami menunggu salam, beliau bertakbir sementara masih duduk dan bersujud dua kali sebelum salam, kemudian memberi salam.'

824.iwh_en: It was narrated from Malik ibn Buhaynah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ stood from two rak'ahs without sitting (for tashahud), and then performed two prostrations (of forgetfulness) before the salam.
824.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Malik ibn Buhaynah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ berdiri dari dua rakaat tanpa duduk (untuk tasyahud), kemudian melakukan dua sujud (sahwi) sebelum salam.

825.iwh_en: It was narrated that when the prayer was finished, the imam gave the salam and prostrated two prostrations of forgetfulness, then gave the salam again, and said: 'This is what the Messenger of Allah ﷺ did with us.'
825.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan bahwa ketika shalat selesai, imam memberi salam dan bersujud dua kali sujud sahwi, kemudian memberi salam lagi, dan berkata: 'Inilah yang Rasulullah ﷺ lakukan bersama kami.'

826.iwh_en: It was narrated from Mu'awiyah ibn al-Hakam (may Allah be pleased with him) that he spoke during the prayer and the people hissed at him to be quiet. When the Messenger of Allah ﷺ finished the prayer, he said: 'By my father and mother, I have never seen a teacher better than him before or after him. He did not hit me, did not scold me, just said: This prayer is not fitting for any human speech — it is only glorification of Allah, magnification of Allah, and recitation of the Quran.'
826.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Mu'awiyah ibn al-Hakam (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berbicara selama shalat dan orang-orang mendesisakannya agar diam. Ketika Rasulullah ﷺ selesai shalat, beliau bersabda: 'Demi ayah dan ibuku, aku tidak pernah melihat seorang guru yang lebih baik darinya sebelum atau sesudahnya. Beliau tidak memukulku, tidak memarahiku, hanya bersabda: Shalat ini tidak cocok untuk percakapan manusia apapun — itu hanya tasbih kepada Allah, pengagungan Allah, dan bacaan Al-Quran.'

827.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ commanded the killing of two black things during prayer: the snake and the scorpion.
827.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ memerintahkan untuk membunuh dua hal hitam selama shalat: ular dan kalajengking.

828.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ya'la ibn Umayyah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he asked about the shortening of prayer mentioned in the Quran: 'If you travel the earth and fear that the disbelievers may harm you' (al-Nisa: 101) — while people are now safe. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'It is a charity that Allah has given you, so accept His charity.'
828.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ya'la ibn Umayyah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia bertanya tentang pemangkasan shalat yang disebutkan dalam Al-Quran: 'Jika kalian bepergian di muka bumi dan kalian khawatir orang-orang kafir akan menyakiti kalian' (al-Nisa: 101) — sementara orang-orang sekarang sudah aman. Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Itu adalah sedekah yang Allah berikan kepada kalian, maka terimalah sedekah-Nya.'

829.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ prayed two rak'ahs at Mina, as did Abu Bakr, Umar, and Uthman for part of his reign.
829.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ shalat dua rakaat di Mina, demikian juga Abu Bakr, Umar, dan Utsman untuk sebagian masa kekhalifahannya.

830.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'We prayed Dhuhr with the Prophet ﷺ at Madinah — four rak'ahs — and prayed with him at Dhu al-Hulayfah — two rak'ahs.'
830.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas ibn Malik (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Kami shalat Dzhuhur bersama Nabi ﷺ di Madinah — empat rakaat — dan shalat bersamanya di Dzu al-Hulayfah — dua rakaat.'

831.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ prayed at Madinah four rak'ahs, and at Dhu al-Hulayfah two rak'ahs.'
831.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas ibn Malik (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ shalat di Madinah empat rakaat, dan di Dzu al-Hulayfah dua rakaat.'

832.iwh_en: It was narrated that the prayer on a journey was established as two rak'ahs, and the prayer at home was completed (to full number). Someone asked: 'Why did she (Aisha) complete the prayer during travel?' He said: 'She made her own interpretation, as Uthman did.'
832.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan bahwa shalat dalam perjalanan ditetapkan sebagai dua rakaat, dan shalat di rumah disempurnakan (menjadi jumlah penuh). Seseorang bertanya: 'Mengapa dia (Aisyah) menyempurnakan shalat selama perjalanan?' Dia berkata: 'Dia membuat penafsirannya sendiri, sebagaimana Utsman lakukan.'

833.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'We went out with the Prophet ﷺ and he continued to shorten the prayer until we arrived at Mecca. He stayed there for ten days, shortening the prayer until he returned.'
833.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas ibn Malik (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Kami keluar bersama Nabi ﷺ dan beliau terus mempersingkat shalat hingga kami tiba di Makkah. Beliau tinggal di sana selama sepuluh hari, mempersingkat shalat hingga beliau kembali.'

834.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Ala' ibn al-Hadhrami (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'The emigrant may stay (in Mecca) three days after completing his pilgrimage rituals.'
834.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Ala' ibn al-Hadhrami (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Orang yang berhijrah boleh tinggal (di Makkah) tiga hari setelah menyelesaikan ritual hajinya.'

835.iwh_en: It was narrated that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ permitted the emigrants to stay three days after the completion of their pilgrimage at Mecca.
835.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ mengizinkan para muhajirin untuk tinggal tiga hari setelah penyelesaian haji mereka di Makkah.

836.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir ibn Abdullah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ prayed fear prayer (salat al-khawf) with one group who came facing the enemy, while another group guarded. He prayed one rak'ah with the first group and they departed; the second group came and he prayed one rak'ah with them; each group completed their prayer on their own.
836.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir ibn Abdullah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ shalat khauf dengan satu kelompok yang datang menghadap musuh, sementara kelompok lain berjaga. Beliau shalat satu rakaat dengan kelompok pertama dan mereka pergi; kelompok kedua datang dan beliau shalat satu rakaat dengan mereka; setiap kelompok menyelesaikan shalat mereka sendiri.

837.iwh_en: It was narrated from Amir ibn Rabi'ah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ performing voluntary prayers while riding his mount, making a head gesture toward whichever direction the animal turned.'
837.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Amir ibn Rabi'ah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku melihat Rasulullah ﷺ mengerjakan shalat sunnah sambil menunggangi kendaraannya, membuat isyarat kepala ke arah manapun binatang itu berpaling.'

838.iwh_en: It was narrated from Mu'adh ibn Jabal (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'We went out with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ in the year of the Tabuk expedition, and he used to combine prayers: he joined Dhuhr and Asr together, and Maghrib and Isha together.'
838.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Mu'adh ibn Jabal (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Kami keluar bersama Rasulullah ﷺ dalam tahun ekspedisi Tabuk, dan beliau biasa menggabungkan shalat: beliau menggabungkan Dzhuhur dan Ashar bersama-sama, dan Maghrib dan Isya bersama-sama.'

839.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ combined Maghrib and Isha on a journey.
839.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ menggabungkan Maghrib dan Isya dalam perjalanan.

840.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to combine Maghrib and Isha when he was in a hurry during travel.
840.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ biasa menggabungkan Maghrib dan Isya ketika beliau tergesa-gesa dalam perjalanan.

841.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ also combined prayers at that place. He said that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ did the same at that place.
841.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ juga menggabungkan shalat di tempat itu. Ia berkata bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ melakukan hal yang sama di tempat itu.

842.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah and Ubaydullah, sons of Ka'b ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with them), that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ combined the two prayers at Tabuk.
842.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah dan Ubaydullah, putra-putra Ka'b ibn Malik (radhiyallahu 'anhuma), bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ menggabungkan dua shalat di Tabuk.

843.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I fought with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ in an expedition toward Najd. We faced the enemy and formed our ranks. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ stood up and led us in prayer of fear.'
843.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku berperang bersama Rasulullah ﷺ dalam ekspedisi menuju Najd. Kami menghadap musuh dan membentuk barisan kami. Rasulullah ﷺ berdiri dan memimpin kami dalam shalat khauf.'

844.iwh_en: It was narrated from Salih ibn Khawwat (may Allah be pleased with him) about the fear prayer (salat al-khawf) that he said: 'The imam prays with one group while another faces the enemy; then he prays one rak'ah with those with him, and they go to stand facing the enemy while the second group comes and he prays the remaining rak'ah with them, and then they complete their prayer on their own.'
844.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Salih ibn Khawwat (radhiyallahu 'anhu) tentang shalat khauf bahwa ia berkata: 'Imam shalat bersama satu kelompok sementara kelompok lain menghadap musuh; kemudian ia shalat satu rakaat bersama mereka yang bersamanya, dan mereka pergi untuk berdiri menghadap musuh sementara kelompok kedua datang dan ia shalat rakaat yang tersisa bersama mereka, dan kemudian mereka menyelesaikan shalat mereka sendiri.'

845.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Sa'id al-Khudri (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'We were detained on the Day of the Trench until deep into the night, when we were spared. This is the saying of Allah: "And Allah saved the believers from fighting" (al-Ahzab: 25). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ ordered Bilal to call the adhan and iqamah, then they prayed Dhuhr, then iqamah for Asr and they prayed it, then Maghrib and Isha — each prayer in its time — and this was before Allah revealed the verse permitting prayer in fear.'
845.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Sa'id al-Khudri (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Kami ditahan pada Hari Parit hingga jauh malam, ketika kami diselamatkan. Ini adalah firman Allah: "Dan Allah menyelamatkan orang-orang mukmin dari pertempuran" (al-Ahzab: 25). Rasulullah ﷺ memerintahkan Bilal untuk mengumandangkan adzan dan iqamah, kemudian mereka shalat Dzhuhur, kemudian iqamah untuk Ashar dan mereka mendirikannya, kemudian Maghrib dan Isya — setiap shalat pada waktunya — dan ini sebelum Allah menurunkan ayat yang mengizinkan shalat dalam ketakutan.'

846.iwh_en: It was narrated from a companion (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The sun and moon do not eclipse on account of the death of any person. They are two of the signs of Allah. When you see them, stand and pray.'
846.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang sahabat (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Matahari dan bulan tidak gerhana karena kematian siapapun. Keduanya adalah dua dari tanda-tanda kebesaran Allah. Apabila kalian melihatnya, berdirilah dan shalatlah.'

847.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him) about the eclipse prayer that it consisted of two rak'ahs, with two bowings in each rak'ah, then the Prophet ﷺ addressed the people and said: 'The sun and moon are two signs from the signs of Allah; they do not eclipse for the death or life of anyone.'
847.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) tentang shalat gerhana bahwa terdiri dari dua rakaat, dengan dua kali ruku di setiap rakaat, kemudian Nabi ﷺ menyampaikan khutbah kepada orang-orang dan bersabda: 'Matahari dan bulan adalah dua tanda dari tanda-tanda kebesaran Allah; keduanya tidak gerhana karena kematian atau kelahiran siapapun.'

848.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that a Jewish woman came to her and mentioned the punishment of the grave, and asked the Prophet ﷺ whether people are punished in their graves. He said: 'I seek refuge with Allah.' Then she heard the Prophet ﷺ thereafter seeking refuge from the punishment of the grave in every prayer.
848.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa seorang perempuan Yahudi datang kepadanya dan menyebutkan azab kubur, dan bertanya kepada Nabi ﷺ apakah orang-orang disiksa di kuburan mereka. Beliau bersabda: 'Aku berlindung kepada Allah.' Kemudian dia mendengar Nabi ﷺ setelah itu memohon perlindungan dari azab kubur dalam setiap shalat.

849.iwh_en: It was narrated from Asma' bint Abi Bakr (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Indeed I see that you will be tried in your graves like the trial of the Dajjal.'
849.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Asma' binti Abi Bakr (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Sesungguhnya aku melihat bahwa kalian akan diuji di kuburan kalian seperti ujian al-Dajjal.'

850.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ saying: 'O Allah, I seek refuge in You from the punishment of the grave, and I seek refuge in You from the punishment of the Fire.'
850.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ mengucapkan: 'Ya Allah, aku berlindung kepada-Mu dari azab kubur, dan aku berlindung kepada-Mu dari azab neraka.'

851.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him) about the eclipse prayer that the Prophet ﷺ prayed two rak'ahs with two bowings in each rak'ah, then addressed the people and said: 'The sun and moon are two signs of Allah; they do not eclipse for the death or life of anyone. When you see them, remember Allah.'
851.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) tentang shalat gerhana bahwa Nabi ﷺ shalat dua rakaat dengan dua kali ruku di setiap rakaat, kemudian menyampaikan khutbah kepada orang-orang dan bersabda: 'Matahari dan bulan adalah dua tanda Allah; keduanya tidak gerhana karena kematian atau kelahiran siapapun. Apabila kalian melihatnya, ingatlah Allah.'

852.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: 'The sun was eclipsed and the Prophet ﷺ prayed — she described that he prayed two rak'ahs with two bowings in each rak'ah.'
852.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: 'Matahari gerhana dan Nabi ﷺ shalat — dia menggambarkan bahwa beliau shalat dua rakaat dengan dua kali ruku di setiap rakaat.'

853.iwh_en: It was narrated from Asma' bint Abi Bakr (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Prophet ﷺ commanded the freeing of slaves during the solar eclipse.
853.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Asma' binti Abi Bakr (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Nabi ﷺ memerintahkan untuk membebaskan budak saat gerhana matahari.

854.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Prophet ﷺ came out to the musalla (open prayer area) seeking rain. He turned his cloak inside out while facing the qiblah, then prayed two rak'ahs.'
854.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Zayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Nabi ﷺ keluar ke mushalla (tempat shalat terbuka) untuk memohon hujan. Beliau membalik jubahnya sementara menghadap kiblat, kemudian shalat dua rakaat.'

855.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abbad ibn Tamim (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ went out with the people to the prayer ground and prayed for rain. He prayed two rak'ahs and turned his cloak inside out.
855.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abbad ibn Tamim (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ keluar bersama orang-orang ke lapangan shalat dan shalat istisqa (memohon hujan). Beliau shalat dua rakaat dan membalik jubahnya.

856.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ never raised his hands in any supplication except in the prayer for rain, and he would raise them until the whiteness of his armpits was seen.'
856.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas ibn Malik (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ tidak pernah mengangkat tangannya dalam doa apapun kecuali dalam shalat istisqa, dan beliau mengangkatnya hingga terlihat putih ketiak beliau.'

857.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'When one of you comes on Friday, let him perform ghusl.'
857.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Apabila salah seorang dari kalian datang pada hari Jumat, hendaklah ia mandi.'

858.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'The ghusl of Friday is obligatory on every adult.'
858.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Mandi Jumat adalah wajib bagi setiap orang yang telah dewasa.'

859.iwh_en: It was narrated that Umar ibn al-Khattab (may Allah be pleased with him) was giving the khutbah when a man entered. Umar criticized him saying: 'What is the matter with you?' He said: 'O Commander of the Faithful, I did nothing except perform wudu when I heard the call.' Umar said: 'And only wudu? Have you not heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say one should perform ghusl on Friday?'
859.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan bahwa Umar ibn al-Khattab (radhiyallahu 'anhu) sedang berkhutbah ketika seorang laki-laki masuk. Umar mengkritiknya mengatakan: 'Ada apa denganmu?' Dia berkata: 'Wahai Amirul Mukminin, aku tidak melakukan apapun kecuali berwudhu ketika mendengar panggilan.' Umar berkata: 'Dan hanya wudhu? Apakah kamu tidak mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda bahwa seseorang harus mandi pada hari Jumat?'

860.iwh_en: It was narrated from Samurah ibn Jundub (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'Whoever performs wudu on Friday — that is well and good. But whoever performs ghusl, the ghusl is better.'
860.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Samurah ibn Jundub (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa yang berwudhu pada hari Jumat — itu baik dan bagus. Tetapi barangsiapa yang mandi, maka mandinya lebih utama.'
